title: Xi set to welcome Trump from a position of strength
subhead: The US president is visiting China from Wednesday, at a time when he is embroiled in the conflict with Tehran, of which Beijing is a partner. Meanwhile, the oil and gas crisis linked to the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has spared China, which had taken steps to protect itself from this type of risk.

title: Assemblée Nationale inquiry commission exposes deep flaws in France's museum security model
At Nairobi summit, France makes push to invest in Africa
Iran says US must accept its peace plan or face 'failure'
subhead: Both sides have refused to make concessions and repeatedly threatened to resume fighting, but neither appears willing to return to all-out war. Iran sent its latest proposal in response to an earlier US plan, details of which remain limited.

title: Stars descend on red carpet as Cannes Film Festival opens without Hollywood backing
subhead: As usual in the build-up to the festival, off-screen events have dominated conversation, from the impact of AI on jobs, Hollywood's decision to ghost the event and the under-representation of women directors.

title: The shattered Iranian lives of Manije, Mohammad-Reza and Soroush, all killed by bombings
subhead: Before the Israeli-US bombings began, they were living as retirees and artists in different parts of Tehran. Until they met their deaths in their own homes. Tormented by distress, their loved ones abroad and in Iran recount their last conversations before fate took a tragic turn.

title: French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot: 'In Africa, as elsewhere in the world, Russia has been largely defeated'
subhead: At the conclusion of the Africa Forward Summit held in Nairobi, France's foreign minister said in an interview with Le Monde that trade and investment between France and Africa far surpasses Moscow's involvement on the continent.

title: Gaza's displaced persons' camps are overrun by rats
Strait of Hormuz: UN seeks to secure fertilizer shipments to fragile countries to prevent famines
US Justice Department warns it will subpoena journalists who receive classified information
October 7: New report documents extent of sexual violence committed by Hamas
UK PM Starmer fights for political survival as calls for his resignation grow
Shipping giant CMA CGM uses its fortune to expand its empire on land

Seine murders: Psychiatric assessment finds suspect cannot be held criminally responsible
Ukraine seeks to broaden diplomatic horizon by selling arms
Epstein files on display at New York pop-up exhibit, all 3.5 million pages
Two killed in shooting as violent turf war erupts over drug dealing hotspots in Nice
Eye Haïdara, master of ceremonies at the Cannes Film Festival: 'When you're on stage, you're in charge! You're untouchable'
French far-right mayors' first steps target Europe, culture and unions
subhead: Le Monde examined the first decisions made by newly-elected far-right mayors. While they have not yet kept their tax promises, almost all of them have increased their own allowances, hired police officers and targeted local figures who are politically hostile to them.

title: Who is Iyad Ag Ghali, the jihadist behind the destabilization of Mali?
subhead: A central figure in Sahelian jihad for nearly two decades, pursued in turn by the French and the Russians, Ghali led the offensive against the junta in Bamako. Since 2012, the jihadist and leader of Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM) has softened his rhetoric, seeking to appear more approachable and less radical.

title: After a decade of crises between France and its former colonies, Macron chooses Kenya for the last major African trip of his presidency
subhead: Coming to power having promised to rebuild the relationship between France and its former colonies, Emmanuel Macron had to face a series of crises, particularly in the Sahel region. In the English-speaking part of the continent, Paris struggled to find its place.

title: 'Israel's impunity in Gaza can only encourage it to be just as ruthless in Lebanon'
Sandra Laugier, philosopher: 'In France as in the US, never has freedom of expression been so often invoked by its enemies'
'The word 'masculinism' is deeply inappropriate and depoliticizes feminism'
